---
# EXECUTION-SPEC EXEC-083
# Generated by build-pipeline via lead-architect (scaffold)
# PRD: docs/10-prd/PRD-083-pilot-auth-containment-v0.md
# FIB-H: docs/issues/gaps/pilot-auth-containment/FIB-H-PILOT-AUTH-CONTAINMENT-001.md
# FIB-S: docs/issues/gaps/pilot-auth-containment/FIB-S-PILOT-AUTH-CONTAINMENT-001.json (manually bound)

id: EXEC-083
prd: PRD-083
prd_title: "Pilot Authentication Containment Gate"
service: PilotContainmentService (operational containment capability)
mvp_phase: pilot-containment
status: Draft
date: 2026-05-12

# Intake Authority Chain
fib_h: docs/issues/gaps/pilot-auth-containment/FIB-H-PILOT-AUTH-CONTAINMENT-001.md
fib_s: docs/issues/gaps/pilot-auth-containment/FIB-S-PILOT-AUTH-CONTAINMENT-001.json
fib_s_loaded: true

# GOV-010 waiver: adr_refs + scaffold_ref absent from PRD frontmatter; ADRs verified
# via affects field and PRD §9 (ADR-015, 018, 024, 030, 041, 044). Bound FIB-S manually
# (file confirmed on disk; section 9 explicit citation). Waiver: ADRs-verified-in-body.

# Complexity: Full path (migrations + RLS + service-role server actions + new operational capability +
# new API surfaces + 10 workstreams)

adr_refs:
  - docs/80-adrs/ADR-015-rls-connection-pooling-strategy.md
  - docs/80-adrs/ADR-018-security-definer-governance.md
  - docs/80-adrs/ADR-024_DECISIONS.md
  - docs/80-adrs/ADR-030-auth-system-hardening.md
  - docs/80-adrs/ADR-035-client-state-lifecycle-auth-transitions.md
  - docs/80-adrs/ADR-041-surface-governance-standard.md
  - docs/80-adrs/ADR-044-testing-governance-posture.md

workstreams:
  WS1:
    name: Containment Schema, RLS, and SRM Registration
    type: database
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Create pilot_access_requests and approved_email_allowlist tables with canonical
      email storage (lower-trimmed). RLS: pilot_access_requests INSERT open to anon,
      SELECT/UPDATE service_role only; approved_email_allowlist SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE
      service_role only. All allowlist checks occur server-side only.
      Enforce canonical email and finite status values at the database layer with
      CHECK constraints. Add lookup indexes for allowlist gate reads and pending
      review queue reads. Treat DB constraints as the source of truth; service
      validation is defense-in-depth only.
      SRM registered as PilotContainmentService v4.24.0.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    traces_to:
      - capability:submit_access_request
      - capability:check_email_allowlist_before_otp
      - capability:approve_pilot_access
      - capability:revoke_pilot_access
      - rule:RULE-1
      - rule:RULE-8
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/{timestamp}_pilot_auth_containment.sql
      - docs/20-architecture/SERVICE_RESPONSIBILITY_MATRIX.md   # SRM v4.24.0 update
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS2:
    name: Pilot Service + Auth Server Actions
    type: service
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Implement services/pilot/ (dtos, schemas, mappers, crud, keys, index) owning
      allowlist gate logic and email canonicalization. Implement two server actions:
      sendMagicLinkAction (allowlist check → signInWithOtp — fail closed if not approved)
      and requestPilotAccessAction (insert pending row — safe duplicate response for
      existing pending email). Both actions must use canonical email form (lowercase trim).
      sendMagicLinkAction must NOT call supabase.auth.signUp() under any branch.
      Because this check runs before authentication exists, the allowlist read uses a
      server-only service-role read helper that returns only approved/not_approved and
      never exposes row details to the client. Admin authority is not required for this
      read-only containment check; admin authority is required for review mutations.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1]
    traces_to:
      - capability:check_email_allowlist_before_otp
      - capability:send_magic_link
      - capability:submit_access_request
      - capability:display_closed_pilot_messaging
      - rule:RULE-1
      - rule:RULE-2
      - rule:RULE-7
    outputs:
      - services/pilot/dtos.ts
      - services/pilot/schemas.ts
      - services/pilot/mappers.ts
      - services/pilot/crud.ts
      - services/pilot/keys.ts
      - services/pilot/index.ts
      - app/actions/auth/send-magic-link.ts
      - app/actions/auth/request-pilot-access.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS3:
    name: Onboarding Mutation Hardening
    type: service
    bounded_context: onboarding
    description: >
      Remove skipAuth: true from registerCompanyAction (app/(onboarding)/register/_actions.ts)
      and bootstrapAction (app/(onboarding)/bootstrap/_actions.ts). Both actions must use
      a dedicated pre-staff onboarding guard: requireApprovedPilotSession().
      The guard guarantees a valid authenticated Supabase session, canonicalized
      allowlisted email, active allowlist status, and no staff binding requirement.
      This guard is valid only for registerCompanyAction and bootstrapAction. Do not
      rely on implicit withAuth behavior or assumptions about staff-binding requirements.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2]
    traces_to:
      - capability:harden_onboarding_mutations
      - rule:RULE-4
    outputs:
      - app/(onboarding)/register/_actions.ts
      - app/(onboarding)/bootstrap/_actions.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: small

  WS4:
    name: Auth Surface Replacement and Route Retirement
    type: frontend
    bounded_context: auth-ui
    description: >
      Create MagicLinkForm component (email-only, calls sendMagicLinkAction, displays
      closed-pilot messaging when not approved). Replace LoginForm in both login surfaces:
      app/(public)/auth/login/page.tsx and app/(marketing)/signin/page.tsx. Replace
      app/(public)/auth/sign-up/page.tsx to render RequestAccessForm in place per DEC-2.
      Add HTTP 307 redirects: /auth/forgot-password → /auth/login and
      /auth/update-password → /auth/login. components/login-form.tsx and
      components/sign-up-form.tsx remain on disk but are no longer imported by any
      public route (mark as deprecated or remove at executor discretion).
      Surface classification (ADR-041) required for magic-link login surfaces.
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2]
    traces_to:
      - capability:disable_public_signup
      - capability:retire_password_routes
      - capability:display_closed_pilot_messaging
      - rule:RULE-2
      - rule:RULE-5
    outputs:
      - components/magic-link-form.tsx
      - app/(public)/auth/login/page.tsx
      - app/(marketing)/signin/page.tsx
      - app/(public)/auth/sign-up/page.tsx
      - app/(public)/auth/forgot-password/page.tsx
      - app/(public)/auth/update-password/page.tsx
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS5:
    name: Request Access Public Surface
    type: frontend
    bounded_context: auth-ui
    description: >
      Create app/(public)/request-access/page.tsx and RequestAccessForm component.
      Form fields: name, email, casino_name, role, estimated_table_count, message (optional).
      Calls requestPilotAccessAction on submit. Displays confirmation messaging on success
      without revealing whether the email was previously submitted. No auth required.
      Surface classification (ADR-041) required: Client Shell rendering (form interaction),
      Simple Query aggregation (no server data at load time).
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2]
    traces_to:
      - capability:submit_access_request
      - rule:RULE-8
    outputs:
      - app/(public)/request-access/page.tsx
      - components/request-access-form.tsx
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: small

  WS6:
    name: Session-Time Pilot Authorization Gates
    type: service
    bounded_context: auth-routing
    description: >
      Extend app/(public)/start/page.tsx to perform an allowlist check between the
      existing auth check (step 1) and the staff-binding check (step 2). The check
      queries approved_email_allowlist through the server-only allowlist read helper
      for user.email (canonical form) and asserts the entry exists and is not revoked.
      Fail closed: if the query errors, the
      entry is missing, or the status is revoked → redirect to /request-access with
      a non-revealing message. Must not restructure the existing /start routing logic
      beyond inserting the containment check. /start remains the canonical post-auth
      router per RULE-6.

      Also enforce the same containment invariant on every authenticated route group
      that can expose operator or onboarding surfaces without traversing /start.
      Direct deep links to app/(dashboard)/*, app/(protected)/*, /register, and
      /bootstrap must not be authorized by authentication alone. Route-group guards
      must reject authenticated but non-allowlisted sessions before rendering page
      content. Onboarding pages may use the pre-staff approved-pilot guard; dashboard
      and protected app shells must require an active staff binding after allowlist
      approval.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2]
    traces_to:
      - capability:validate_allowlist_at_session_time
      - rule:RULE-3
      - rule:RULE-6
    outputs:
      - app/(public)/start/page.tsx
      - app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx
      - app/(protected)/layout.tsx
      - app/(onboarding)/register/page.tsx
      - app/(onboarding)/bootstrap/page.tsx
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS7:
    name: Pilot Review Server Actions
    type: service
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Implement app/actions/pilot/review-actions.ts with three actions:
      approvePilotAccessAction, rejectPilotAccessAction, revokePilotAccessAction.
      Each action must use a dedicated auth-only guard: requirePilotAdminSession().
      Do not wrap these actions in the staff-bound withServerAction middleware before
      admin authority is resolved. Admin authority pattern: verify authenticated
      session email against PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS env var (comma-separated). Service-role
      client creation is allowed only after requirePilotAdminSession() succeeds. On approval: upsert
      approved_email_allowlist entry (status=active) + update request status=approved.
      On rejection: update request status=rejected only (no allowlist mutation).
      On revoke: update approved_email_allowlist status=revoked. All three are idempotent
      on retry and approval must update request status + allowlist row in one atomic
      operation. Log all outcomes (approval, rejection, revocation, non-admin denial)
      through explicit structured telemetry events with email, actor email, action,
      result, and correlationId — no secrets in log output. Middleware audit is not
      sufficient here because the review flow is intentionally auth-only until admin
      authority is established.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1, WS2]
    traces_to:
      - capability:approve_pilot_access
      - capability:revoke_pilot_access
      - rule:RULE-9
    outputs:
      - app/actions/pilot/review-actions.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS8:
    name: Internal Pilot Review Surface
    type: frontend
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Create app/(internal)/pilot-review/page.tsx as an RSC that fetches pending
      pilot_access_requests server-side (service-role read, admin-only). Renders a
      table of pending requests with submitter details. Approve and reject actions
      invoke approvePilotAccessAction / rejectPilotAccessAction as server actions.
      Admin authority check happens at the page-render level. If unauthenticated,
      redirect to /signin. If authenticated but non-admin, redirect to /request-access
      with non-revealing messaging; do not send authenticated non-admin users back to
      signin. The surface must not exceed operational
      moderation scope per RULE-9. Surface classification (ADR-041) required: RSC Prefetch rendering
      (server-authoritative list required before any interaction), Simple Query
      aggregation (single table, service-role read, no cross-context aggregation).
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS7]
    traces_to:
      - capability:review_access_requests
      - capability:approve_pilot_access
      - capability:revoke_pilot_access
      - rule:RULE-9
    outputs:
      - app/(internal)/pilot-review/page.tsx
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS9:
    name: Unit and Integration Tests
    type: testing
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Unit tests: allowlist gate logic (email not found → rejected, active → approved,
      revoked → rejected, query error → rejected), email canonicalization, duplicate
      request safe response. Integration tests: requestPilotAccess insert + idempotency,
      sendMagicLink allowlist enforcement (OTP not issued for non-approved email),
      approval/rejection/revocation state transitions, non-admin denial for review actions
      (verify DEC-1: admin authority via env var and auth-only admin path without
      staff binding), review telemetry emission (DEC-8), and atomic approval state
      transition. Mutation hardening: verify
      registerCompanyAction and bootstrapAction reject unauthenticated calls and
      reject authenticated but non-allowlisted sessions. DB invariant tests must verify
      canonical-email CHECK constraints, status CHECK constraints, pending duplicate
      uniqueness, allowlist lookup index presence, and pending-review index presence.
      Testing tier: Required (ADR-044 §5 — new operational capability with write paths).
    executor: qa-specialist
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2, WS3, WS6, WS7]
    traces_to:
      - rule:RULE-1
      - rule:RULE-3
      - rule:RULE-4
      - rule:RULE-8
      - rule:RULE-9
      - outcome:OUT-1
      - outcome:OUT-4
      - outcome:OUT-6
    outputs:
      - services/pilot/__tests__/allowlist-gate.test.ts
      - services/pilot/__tests__/email-canonicalization.test.ts
      - app/actions/auth/__tests__/send-magic-link.test.ts
      - app/actions/auth/__tests__/request-pilot-access.test.ts
      - app/actions/pilot/__tests__/review-actions.test.ts
      - app/(onboarding)/register/__tests__/_actions.test.ts
      - app/(onboarding)/bootstrap/__tests__/_actions.test.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS10:
    name: E2E Write-Path Tests
    type: testing
    bounded_context: pilot-containment
    description: >
      Playwright E2E specs covering write-path user journeys. Required coverage:
      request-access form submission (happy path + duplicate email safe response),
      unapproved email login attempt (no OTP, closed-pilot message displayed),
      retired signup route no longer exposes signUp and renders request-access form,
      retired forgot-password route redirects to /auth/login.
      Direct deep-link containment coverage is required for /pit, /shift-dashboard,
      /register, and /bootstrap with an authenticated but non-allowlisted session.
      OTP issuance verification: server-action level (mock signInWithOtp call confirmed
      for approved email) — full magic-link email click deferred per PRD §7.2 risk note.
      Approved pre-staff onboarding path: covered at server action level where feasible.
      E2E mandate triggered by write-path signals: requestPilotAccess (INSERT), approve/
      reject/revoke (UPDATE+UPSERT), /start allowlist check (server-only allowlist read with
      redirect consequence).
    executor: e2e-testing
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS3, WS4, WS5, WS6, WS8]
    traces_to:
      - outcome:OUT-1
      - outcome:OUT-2
      - outcome:OUT-3
      - outcome:OUT-5
      - loop:STEP-1
      - loop:STEP-2
      - loop:STEP-3
      - loop:STEP-6
      - loop:STEP-9
    outputs:
      - e2e/pilot-auth/request-access.spec.ts
      - e2e/pilot-auth/unapproved-login-rejection.spec.ts
      - e2e/pilot-auth/retired-routes.spec.ts
      - e2e/pilot-auth/direct-deeplink-containment.spec.ts
    gate: e2e-write-path
    estimated_complexity: medium

execution_phases:
  - phase: 1
    name: Schema Foundation
    parallel: [WS1]
    description: Schema, RLS, and SRM registration. Blocks all subsequent work.

  - phase: 2
    name: Service Layer
    parallel: [WS2]
    description: >
      Pilot service + auth actions (WS2) establish the allowlist helper and
      pre-staff approved-pilot guard used by downstream workstreams.

  - phase: 3
    name: Mutation Hardening, Surfaces, and Gateway
    parallel: [WS3, WS4, WS5, WS6, WS7]
    description: >
      All surface and gateway workstreams depend on WS2. Auth surface replacement
      (WS4), request-access page (WS5), /start gateway extension (WS6), pilot
      review server actions (WS7), and onboarding mutation hardening (WS3) run in
      parallel after WS2.

  - phase: 4
    name: Admin Review Surface
    parallel: [WS8]
    description: >
      Internal pilot review UI depends on WS7 (review server actions). Sequential
      after Phase 3.

  - phase: 5
    name: Quality Gates
    parallel: [WS9, WS10]
    description: >
      Unit/integration tests (WS9) and E2E write-path tests (WS10) run in parallel
      after all implementation workstreams complete.

gates:
  schema-validation:
    description: DB types regenerated, RLS policies validate, migration applies cleanly
    commands:
      - npm run db:types-local
      - npm test schema-verification
      - npm run type-check

  schema-verification:
    description: Explicit schema verification for SQL migrations and RLS posture
    commands:
      - npm test schema-verification

  type-check:
    description: TypeScript strict check passes for implementation workstreams
    commands:
      - npm run type-check

  test-pass:
    description: Targeted unit and integration tests pass for changed services and actions
    commands:
      - npm run test -- --testPathPattern="services/pilot|actions/auth|actions/pilot|onboarding"

  schema-green:
    description: DB types regenerated, RLS policies validate, migration applies cleanly
    commands:
      - npm run db:types-local
      - npm test schema-verification
      - npm run type-check

  service-green:
    description: Allowlist gate logic unit-tested, type-check passes, no console.* in production code
    commands:
      - npm run type-check
      - npm run test -- --testPathPattern="services/pilot"

  surfaces-green:
    description: >
      No password login path reachable from any public route. Auth surfaces render
      magic-link form only. Redirect targets verified. /start fails closed on
      non-approved session. Direct deep links to dashboard/protected/onboarding
      surfaces fail closed for authenticated but non-allowlisted sessions.
    commands:
      - npm run type-check
      - npm run lint

  review-green:
    description: >
      Non-admin denial tested. Auth-only pilot admin path tested without requiring
      active staff binding. Approval idempotency and atomicity tested. Revocation
      blocks future /start progression. Service-role client never created before
      admin authority confirmed. Review telemetry events emitted for success and
      denial outcomes.
    commands:
      - npm run type-check
      - npm run test -- --testPathPattern="actions/pilot"

  qa-green:
    description: All unit and integration tests pass. No skipAuth bypasses in onboarding actions.
    commands:
      - npm run test:ci

  e2e-write-path:
    description: >
      Write-path E2E specs pass. Request-access, rejection, retired-routes, and
      direct deep-link containment covered.
    commands:
      - npx playwright test e2e/pilot-auth/ --reporter=list

  dod:
    description: Full Definition of Done from PRD §8 satisfied. All gate commands green.
    commands:
      - npm run type-check
      - npm run lint
      - npm run test:ci
      - npx playwright test e2e/pilot-auth/ --reporter=list
      - npm run build

# Open question dispositions (from FIB-S governance.open_questions_allowed_at_scaffold)
decisions:
  - id: DEC-1
    question: "Admin authority mechanism for internal review surface"
    decision: >
      Admin authority is determined server-side by checking the authenticated session
      email against a PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS environment variable (comma-separated list).
      Review actions use a dedicated auth-only requirePilotAdminSession() guard, not
      the existing staff-bound withServerAction chain. No new database column or role
      is introduced. Fail closed: missing env var means no admin access.
    rationale: >
      Minimal, pilot-appropriate pattern. No database change required. Env var is
      server-only and never exposed to client. Supports single-owner pilot without
      building RBAC infrastructure or requiring the application owner to already have
      an active staff binding.
    verification: assumption
    test_requirement: >
      Verify DEC-1: test that review actions reject callers whose session email is
      not in PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS. Test with env var unset (should deny all), single
      email match, non-matching email, and matching admin email with no staff binding.

  - id: DEC-2
    question: "Whether /auth/sign-up is redirected to /request-access or replaced in place"
    decision: >
      Replace in place: app/(public)/auth/sign-up/page.tsx renders RequestAccessForm
      directly. The route continues to exist at /auth/sign-up but no longer calls
      signUp(). This avoids a broken-link scenario for any bookmarked URLs while
      satisfying RULE-2.
    rationale: >
      A redirect loop risk exists if external links point to /auth/sign-up. Replacing
      in place preserves the URL while eliminating the signUp() call.
    verification: cited
    test_requirement: >
      Verify DEC-2: E2E test confirms /auth/sign-up renders request-access form and
      never calls supabase.auth.signUp().

  - id: DEC-3
    question: "Whether expires_at enforcement is activated in this slice"
    decision: >
      expires_at enforcement is deferred and the expires_at column is not created in
      this slice. Allowlist check uses status = 'active' only.
    rationale: >
      PRD §4.2 and FIB-S timing_constraints both confirm expires_at is optional for
      this slice. Adding lifecycle columns now adds coupling without a concrete pilot
      requirement.
    verification: assumption
    test_requirement: >
      Verify DEC-3: approved_email_allowlist contains no expires_at column and the
      allowlist gate approves only status='active'.

  - id: DEC-4
    question: "Whether /auth/update-password and /auth/forgot-password are hard-deleted or soft-redirected"
    decision: >
      Soft-redirect: both pages return a Next.js redirect() to /auth/login. The page
      files remain on disk as redirect stubs. Hard deletion risks 404 for any users
      who have the URL bookmarked or in email history.
    rationale: >
      Safe for pilot. No password infrastructure is active; the redirect prevents
      user confusion while preserving the route file for future clean-up.
    verification: cited
    test_requirement: >
      Verify DEC-4: E2E test confirms /auth/forgot-password and /auth/update-password
      redirect to /auth/login (307 or Next.js redirect behavior).

  - id: DEC-5
    question: "Whether /start is the only session-time containment gate"
    decision: >
      False. /start remains the canonical post-auth router, but it is not the only
      enforcement point. Every route group that can render operator or onboarding
      state must enforce pilot containment independently because authenticated users
      can deep-link around /start.
    rationale: >
      Current route layouts protect dashboard/protected surfaces with auth presence
      checks only. Relying on /start alone leaves a direct deep-link bypass.
    verification: cited
    test_requirement: >
      Verify DEC-5: E2E tests confirm authenticated but non-allowlisted sessions are
      redirected away from /pit, /shift-dashboard, /register, and /bootstrap.

  - id: DEC-6
    question: "How the first pilot admin logs in before allowlist rows exist"
    decision: >
      The rollout requires manual seed of at least one PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS address into
      approved_email_allowlist with status='active' before enabling the public magic-link
      surface in production. Missing seed is a deployment blocker.
    rationale: >
      PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS authorizes review mutations but does not bypass the pilot
      login allowlist. Keeping login and admin authority separate prevents an env var
      from becoming an implicit auth bypass.
    verification: assumption
    test_requirement: >
      Verify DEC-6: deployment checklist confirms every configured pilot admin email
      can pass sendMagicLinkAction and /start before review surface smoke testing.

  - id: DEC-7
    question: "What enforces canonical email and status invariants"
    decision: >
      The database is the source of truth. The migration must add CHECK constraints
      for lower(trim(email)) storage and finite status values on both containment
      tables. Service schemas must mirror these constraints but cannot replace them.
    rationale: >
      Service-only canonicalization can be bypassed by service-role scripts, manual
      operations, or future maintenance paths.
    verification: assumption
    test_requirement: >
      Verify DEC-7: schema verification tests reject mixed-case/space-padded emails
      and invalid statuses when inserted directly.

  - id: DEC-8
    question: "Where pilot review audit records are emitted"
    decision: >
      Pilot review actions emit explicit structured telemetry events for approval,
      rejection, revocation, and non-admin denial. The event type namespace is
      pilot_review.{action}.{result}. Required fields: canonical target email,
      actor email, action, result, and correlationId. Do not rely on withServerAction
      audit middleware for these actions because review authority is auth-only before
      service-role escalation.
    rationale: >
      Existing audit middleware is staff/RLS-context oriented. Pilot review must be
      operable before an admin necessarily has a staff binding.
    verification: assumption
    test_requirement: >
      Verify DEC-8: tests assert telemetry emit calls for approve/reject/revoke
      success, retry/idempotent success, and non-admin denial.

write_path_classification: detected
---

# EXEC-083 — Pilot Authentication Containment Gate

> Implementation spec. Allowed to churn. Changes here must not invalidate PRD-083 or the FIB-S capability inventory.

## 1. Implementation Overview

This EXEC-SPEC implements the minimal pilot authentication containment gate defined in PRD-083 and FIB-S-PILOT-AUTH-CONTAINMENT-001.

The implementation has five distinct concerns:

1. **Schema** — two new containment tables (`pilot_access_requests`, `approved_email_allowlist`) with appropriate RLS postures
2. **Service layer** — `PilotContainmentService` owning the allowlist gate, email canonicalization, and server actions for request submission and magic-link dispatch
3. **Auth surface replacement** — retire password login, sign-up, forgot-password, and update-password public paths; replace with magic-link login and request-access form
4. **Gateway hardening** — allowlist check in `/start` and `skipAuth` removal from onboarding mutations
5. **Internal review** — server actions and UI surface for admin approval/rejection/revocation, constrained to operational moderation per RULE-9

**Core invariant (FIB-H):** If authentication alone can create an operator environment, the pilot boundary is broken.

**PRD:** `docs/10-prd/PRD-083-pilot-auth-containment-v0.md`
**ADRs:** ADR-015, ADR-018, ADR-024, ADR-030, ADR-035, ADR-041, ADR-044

---

## 2. New Operational Capability — PilotContainmentService

### SRM Registration (v4.24.0)

```
Service:  PilotContainmentService
Domain:   Pilot Containment (operational containment capability)
Status:   ✅ IMPLEMENTED (PRD-083, pilot scope)
Tables:   pilot_access_requests, approved_email_allowlist
Server actions:
  sendMagicLinkAction — allowlist check + signInWithOtp
  requestPilotAccessAction — public pending-request insert
  approvePilotAccessAction — service-role, admin-only
  rejectPilotAccessAction  — service-role, admin-only
  revokePilotAccessAction  — service-role, admin-only
RLS posture:
  pilot_access_requests: INSERT open to anon; SELECT/UPDATE service_role only
  approved_email_allowlist: SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE service_role only
Pre-OTP allowlist read: server-only service-role read helper, read-only, result-shaped
                        to approved/not_approved only
Authority: Admin identity via PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS env var (server-only)
```

This service is a pilot containment primitive. It must not absorb identity management,
billing, CRM, or RBAC concerns. See RULE-9.

---

## 3. Database Design

### 3.1 Tables

#### `pilot_access_requests`

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `id` | `uuid` default `gen_random_uuid()` | PK |
| `email` | `text not null` | canonical form (lowercase, trimmed) |
| `name` | `text not null` | submitter name |
| `casino_name` | `text not null` | property / casino name |
| `role` | `text not null` | submitter's role |
| `estimated_table_count` | `int` | |
| `message` | `text` | optional |
| `status` | `text not null default 'pending'` | enum: pending / approved / rejected |
| `reviewed_by` | `text` | nullable — admin email reference (not FK) |
| `reviewed_at` | `timestamptz` | nullable |
| `created_at` | `timestamptz not null default now()` | |

Unique constraint: `UNIQUE(email)` on `(email)` where `status = 'pending'` — partial unique index prevents duplicate pending requests for the same canonical email. Approved/rejected records are preserved for provenance.

Required constraints and indexes:
- `CHECK (email = lower(btrim(email)))`
- `CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected'))`
- `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... ON pilot_access_requests (email) WHERE status = 'pending'`
- `CREATE INDEX ... ON pilot_access_requests (status, created_at) WHERE status = 'pending'`

#### `approved_email_allowlist`

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `id` | `uuid` default `gen_random_uuid()` | PK |
| `email` | `text not null unique` | canonical form (lowercase, trimmed) |
| `status` | `text not null default 'active'` | enum: active / revoked |
| `invited_by` | `text` | nullable — admin email who approved |
| `created_at` | `timestamptz not null default now()` | |

Required constraints and indexes:
- `CHECK (email = lower(btrim(email)))`
- `CHECK (status IN ('active', 'revoked'))`
- `UNIQUE(email)`
- `CREATE INDEX ... ON approved_email_allowlist (email, status)`

No other columns are included in this slice.

### 3.2 RLS Postures

```sql
-- pilot_access_requests
-- Anon can INSERT (public request submission)
-- No public SELECT (prevents enumeration of other requests)
-- UPDATE (status transitions) is service_role only

-- approved_email_allowlist
-- service_role only for SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE
-- all allowlist checks occur server-side only
```

### 3.3 Email Canonicalization Invariant

All email values written to both tables MUST be `lower(trim(email))`. The allowlist gate function must canonicalize its input before querying. The uniqueness constraints are case-insensitive by construction (canonical form is the key).

---

## 4. Auth Flow Architecture

### 4.1 Pre-OTP Containment (RULE-1, RULE-2, RULE-5)

```
User submits email → sendMagicLinkAction (server)
  1. Canonicalize email
  2. Server-only service-role read of approved_email_allowlist
     WHERE email = canonical AND status = 'active'
  3. Not found / revoked → return AllowlistResult.not_approved
     (no OTP, no auth user interaction, no technical details in response)
  4. Found (active) → supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({ email: canonical })
  5. Return AllowlistResult.approved (OTP sent)
```

### 4.2 Session-Time Containment (RULE-3, RULE-6)

```
/start page (RSC) — augmented check sequence:
  1. supabase.auth.getUser() — if no session → redirect /signin
  2. [NEW] server-only allowlist read helper query for canonical(user.email)
     → not found / revoked / query error → redirect /request-access
  3. existing: staff binding check → registration check → casino setup → /pit
```

The allowlist gate is inserted between steps 1 and 2 (auth and staff checks). The existing routing logic is unchanged after the gate passes.

### 4.2.1 Direct Deep-Link Containment (DEC-5)

`/start` is not a complete perimeter. Authenticated users can request protected
routes directly. Therefore:

- `app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx` must require approved pilot authorization and an active staff binding before rendering operator surfaces.
- `app/(protected)/layout.tsx` must require approved pilot authorization and an active staff binding before rendering protected app surfaces.
- `app/(onboarding)/register/page.tsx` and `app/(onboarding)/bootstrap/page.tsx` must require approved pilot authorization before rendering pre-staff onboarding forms.
- Authenticated but non-allowlisted sessions redirect to `/request-access`.
- Authenticated and allowlisted but non-staff users may render onboarding pages only; they must not render dashboard/protected app shells.

### `/start` Constraint

`/start` remains a transitional routing surface only.

It must not evolve into:
- generalized authorization engine
- billing gate
- feature-flag router
- tenant-state orchestrator
- subscription policy layer
- generalized workflow engine

Its responsibility is limited to:
- auth presence validation
- pilot authorization validation
- onboarding progression routing

### 4.3 Mutation Hardening (RULE-4)

`registerCompanyAction` and `bootstrapAction` currently pass `skipAuth: true` to `withServerAction`. This bypasses the auth middleware chain (`withAuth` + `withRLS`). The hardening removes this option and replaces implicit auth assumptions with a dedicated `requireApprovedPilotSession()` guard for these two actions only.

### 4.3.1 Pre-Staff Authorization Contract

Pre-staff onboarding is a distinct authorization state.

The following states are intentionally separate:

authenticated session
≠
pilot authorization
≠
staff binding

`requireApprovedPilotSession()` exists specifically to preserve approved first-login onboarding before staff creation occurs.

This contract must remain explicit. Future auth refactors must not reintroduce implicit staff-binding requirements into onboarding mutations.

### 4.4 Admin Authority (DEC-1)

```
PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS=admin@example.com,owner@example.com  (env var, server-only)

review-actions.ts:
  async function requirePilotAdminSession(supabase) {
    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
    if (!user) throw new UnauthorizedError('unauthenticated');
    const adminEmails = (process.env.PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS ?? '')
      .split(',').map(e => e.trim().toLowerCase()).filter(Boolean);
    if (!adminEmails.includes(user.email?.toLowerCase() ?? '')) {
      throw new UnauthorizedError('not_admin');
    }
    return user;
  }
  // createServiceRoleClient() is only called AFTER requirePilotAdminSession resolves.
  // Do not use staff-bound withServerAction before this guard.
```

Review outcomes emit explicit telemetry events per DEC-8:
`pilot_review.approve.success`, `pilot_review.reject.success`,
`pilot_review.revoke.success`, and `pilot_review.{action}.denied`.
Approval must persist allowlist upsert and request status update atomically.

---

## 5. Surface Classification Declarations (ADR-041)

### 5.1 Magic-Link Login (`/auth/login`, `/signin`)

| Axis | Classification | §4 Criteria |
|------|---------------|-------------|
| Rendering Delivery | Client Shell | Q1: form interaction + optimistic pending state; no server data to prefetch |
| Data Aggregation | Simple Query | Q2: email-only submit; no server data consumed at load time |
| Rejected: RSC Prefetch | — | §4.1: no server-authoritative read required before form render |
| Rejected: BFF RPC | — | §4.2: no cross-context aggregation; single action call |
| Metric Provenance | None | No truth-bearing metrics rendered |

Executor (WS4 — frontend-design-pt-2) must complete full ADR-041 §5 declaration in component file.

### 5.2 Request-Access Page (`/request-access`)

| Axis | Classification | §4 Criteria |
|------|---------------|-------------|
| Rendering Delivery | Client Shell | Q1: multi-field form with optimistic feedback; no server read at load |
| Data Aggregation | Simple Query | Q2: submit-only; no aggregated data consumed |
| Rejected: RSC Prefetch | — | §4.1: pure form submission, nothing to prefetch |
| Rejected: BFF RPC | — | §4.2: single INSERT action, no aggregation |
| Metric Provenance | None | No truth-bearing metrics rendered |

### 5.3 Internal Pilot Review (`/pilot-review`)

| Axis | Classification | §4 Criteria |
|------|---------------|-------------|
| Rendering Delivery | RSC Prefetch | Q1: server-authoritative list required before any interaction; admin reads before acting |
| Data Aggregation | Simple Query | Q2: single table query (service_role read); no cross-context join |
| Rejected: Client Shell | — | §4.1: no progressive enhancement; full data required at render |
| Rejected: BFF RPC | — | §4.2: no summarization or multi-context aggregation |
| Metric Provenance | None | No truth-bearing metrics rendered |

---

## 6. Testing Governance (ADR-044)

**New operational capability: PilotContainmentService** requires testing tier declaration:

| Layer | Tier | Scope |
|-------|------|-------|
| Unit | Required | Allowlist gate logic, email canonicalization, duplicate request safe response |
| Integration | Required | Server actions (request submission, OTP rejection, approval/revocation/revoke), auth-only admin path, non-admin denial, review telemetry, DB invariant constraints |
| E2E | Required | Write-path mandate: request-access submit, unapproved login rejection, retired routes, direct deep-link containment |

E2E mandate triggered: write paths detected (INSERT into pilot_access_requests, UPSERT into approved_email_allowlist, redirect consequences in /start).

---

## 7. Security Posture

| Control | Implementation | Reference |
|---------|---------------|-----------|
| signUp() disabled | /auth/sign-up renders RequestAccessForm only | RULE-2 |
| Allowlist before OTP | sendMagicLinkAction gate (server-side, fail closed) | RULE-1 |
| Session-time authorization | /start allowlist check before staff routing | RULE-3 |
| Direct route containment | Dashboard/protected/onboarding route groups reject non-allowlisted sessions | DEC-5 |
| Mutation session enforcement | skipAuth removed from register + bootstrap actions | RULE-4 |
| Password login retired | All pilot-facing surfaces use MagicLinkForm only | RULE-5 |
| Service-role guard | requirePilotAdminSession() called before createServiceRoleClient() | RULE-9 |
| Review audit sink | Explicit `pilot_review.*` telemetry emitted for approvals, rejections, revocations, and denials | DEC-8 |
| Non-revealing errors | MagicLinkForm message: "Enter your email to check access" / "Request access" only | RULE-7 |
| Error detail safety | All DomainErrors use safeErrorDetails() — no raw Error objects | INV-ERR-DETAILS |
| Email canonicalization | DB CHECK constraints and all writes/queries use lower(trim(email)) | DEC-7 |
| Admin bootstrap | At least one PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS address is manually seeded as active before production enablement | DEC-6 |

---

## 8. Known Gaps / Deviations

| PRD Specification | Implementation | Rationale |
|-------------------|---------------|-----------|
| "optionally record notes" on approval | Notes field deferred | No FIB-S capability for notes; DEC-1 keeps review surface minimal |
| expires_at enforcement | Column and enforcement deferred | DEC-3 — deferred per FIB-S timing_constraints and PRD §4.2 |
| Full magic-link E2E (email click) | Server-action level only | PRD §7.2 risk note — external email interception not feasible in CI |

---

## 9. Rollout Plan

- [ ] Migration applied (`npm run db:migrate` local, then remote)
- [ ] Types regenerated (`npm run db:types-local`)
- [ ] DB constraints verified: canonical email CHECKs, status CHECKs, pending duplicate uniqueness, allowlist lookup index, pending-review index
- [ ] `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` env var set in Vercel and local `.env.local`
- [ ] At least one `PILOT_ADMIN_EMAILS` address manually seeded into `approved_email_allowlist` with `status='active'`
- [ ] Seeded pilot admin can receive magic link and pass `/start`
- [ ] Supabase OTP email delivery verified operational
- [ ] SRM updated to v4.24.0
- [ ] All gate commands green
- [ ] Manual smoke test: unapproved email → no OTP; approved email → OTP received
- [ ] Manual smoke test: authenticated non-allowlisted session deep-linking to `/pit`, `/shift-dashboard`, `/register`, and `/bootstrap` is redirected to `/request-access`
